talk_metadata.talk_title: Biparatopic antibodies targeting FGFR2 fusions and ADC development
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we see striking absence of FGFR2 on the surface"
Speaker said: "we think the activity was primarily driven by loss of FGFR2 through internalization and degradation"
Speaker said: "the small molecule inhibitor gave about -20% tumor regression, not as good as the small molecule, but it's also not so far off"
Speaker said: "we got pretty good degradation without having to do any additional manipulation of the antibody"

vulnerability_identified.failing_compound_or_target: Biparatopic FGFR2 antibodies (e.g., BD, BC) as monotherapy
vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Inability to fully regress tumors despite internalization; unclear why some biparatopic formats become agonists in ligand‑independent settings
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: Speaker said: "we were unable to disrupt the dimers, which were our original hypothesis"
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Speaker said: "antibodies BD and BC had significant inhibition of tumor growth, though not regression"

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker disclosed that only 15 biparatopic combinations were sampled from a very small diversity space, suggesting limited breadth of the platform
competitive_moat_weakness.scale_or_manufacturing_bottleneck: No explicit mention, but extensive ADC conjugation work suggests added complexity
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on naked antibody activity that is inferior to small‑molecule FGFR inhibitors

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that biparatopic antibodies cause robust FGFR2 internalization and degradation (mechanistic observation) but concluded that the antibodies are "not as good as the small molecule" (contradictory efficacy conclusion)

key_data_gaps: No in‑human efficacy or safety data presented
Lack of comparative pharmacokinetic or toxicity profiling versus small‑molecule FGFR inhibitors

crispro_opportunity.description: FGFR2 fusion status and kinase‑domain mutation status are not used to select patients in the preclinical models, representing a gap for CrisPRO predictive biomarker integration.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we thought that perhaps these antibodies would also work in inhibitor‑resistant settings" and created kinase‑domain resistant mutations for testing.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model the impact of specific FGFR2 fusions and resistance mutations on antibody binding and internalization to prioritize candidates.
external_validation_needed: ClinicalTrials.gov: check any FGFR2‑targeted bispecific trials for biomarker criteria
PubMed: resistance mechanisms for FGFR2 inhibitors

crispro_opportunity.description: Biparatopic antibodies retain activity against FGFR2 kinase‑domain resistance mutations, suggesting a combinatorial strategy with FGFR inhibitors.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "BC and BD have anti‑proliferative activity for both of the intracellular kinase domain mutations".
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can predict synergistic combinations of biparatopic antibodies with FGFR inhibitors and forecast resistance trajectories.
external_validation_needed: Preclinical data on FGFR inhibitor + biparatopic antibody synergy
Patent filings on FGFR2 biparatopic formats

crispro_opportunity.description: Combining biparatopic FGFR2 antibodies with small‑molecule FGFR inhibitors may overcome residual tumor growth.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "the antibody plus the small molecule might end up to be a way to defeat resistance in a combination strategy".
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate pharmacodynamic interactions and optimal dosing schedules for antibody‑small molecule combos.
external_validation_needed: Phase I/II trial designs involving FGFR2 antibodies + FGFR inhibitors

crispro_opportunity.description: Ribosome‑display nanobody platform enables rapid generation of diverse binders, addressing the limited combinatorial space of traditional biparatopic screening.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we screened three different libraries, each 10^11 in size, and after six cycles we had 500,000 unique sequences".
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can prioritize nanobody clusters based on predicted epitope complementarity and developability, accelerating lead selection.
external_validation_needed: Benchmarking of ribosome‑display output against conventional hybridoma libraries

cited_competitors.name: Small‑molecule FGFR inhibitors
cited_competitors.context: Compared as a benchmark for tumor regression efficacy

unresolved_questions: Why do certain biparatopic antibodies become agonists in the absence of ligand?
Can the internalization‑driven degradation be quantitatively linked to in‑vivo tumor regression?

assets_to_track: FGFR2 biparatopic antibodies BD and BC
FGFR2 ADC candidates (MMAF, MMAE, deruxtecan conjugates)

talk_metadata.talk_title: Zenitatamab (zanidatamab) – a biparatopic HER2 antibody and trispecific costimulatory antibodies
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Zani mediates very potent CDC, whereas the individual antibodies and the combination did not"
Speaker said: "zenatuximab meets full tumor regression at closely equivalent or even lesser dosing"
Speaker said: "the progression‑free survival and overall survival benefit was observed independent of PD‑L1 status"
Speaker said: "the dual scFv format showed loss of growth inhibitory potential and potential agonism"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Spatial distance between antigen‑binding domains leading to unintended HER2 agonism
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Speaker said: "dual scFv format showed loss of growth inhibitory potential and potential agonism"
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Speaker said: "we observed agonism occurring in this growth inhibition assay"

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: HER2‑positive biliary tract and gastric cancers
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: No concurrent standard‑of‑care arm (chemo + trastuzumab + PD‑L1) for direct comparative efficacy
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: PD‑L1 status not used for stratification despite being a known predictor for HER2‑targeted combos
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Cross‑trial comparison may overstate benefit without a head‑to‑head control

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: Biparatopic HER2 antibody (zenitatamab) binding in trans
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker disclosed that the trans binding leads to HER2 clustering, a mechanism that could be mimicked by engineered bispecifics from competitors
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on a single HER2 epitope combination may limit efficacy against HER2‑heterogeneous tumors

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker noted that paratope positioning dramatically affects affinity and target dependency, indicating a narrow design space that competitors could exploit
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Potential for peripheral T‑cell activation if target dependency is not strictly enforced

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that zenitatamab creates large HER2 clusters enabling CDC (scientific observation) but also stated that the antibody’s overall antitumor activity is comparable to the trastuzumab + pertuzumab combo in many settings (contradictory conclusion)
Speaker demonstrated that trispecifics can provide superior T‑cell activation (observation) yet admitted that most trispecifics in the clinic have shown limited antitumor activity (contradiction)

key_data_gaps: No overall‑survival data from a randomized control arm
Limited biomarker‑stratified analysis beyond HER2 IHC‑3+

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify HER2 expression patterns or co‑mutations that predict the CDC‑mediated advantage of zenitatamab over trastuzumab + pertuzumab.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "zenitatamab mediates very potent CDC, whereas the individual antibodies and the combination did not"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate HER2 epitope mapping with CDC assay data to define a predictive biomarker panel.
external_validation_needed: ClinicalTrials.gov: retrieve HER2 IHC and CDC assay data from zenitatamab trials
PubMed: studies linking HER2 clustering to complement activation

crispro_opportunity.description: Explore whether HER2‑negative or low‑expressing tumor cells become susceptible via the bystander effect observed with zenitatamab.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we see robust killing of HER2‑negative cells in the admixed model"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model diffusion of complement components and predict bystander cytotoxicity across heterogeneous tumor populations.
external_validation_needed: In‑vivo xenograft studies with mixed HER2‑positive/negative cell populations

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine zenitatamab with checkpoint inhibitors (e.g., tislelizumab) to exploit the observed PD‑L1‑independent survival benefit.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "progression‑free and overall survival benefit was observed independent of PD‑L1 status"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate immune‑cell recruitment and predict synergistic effects of CDC‑active antibodies with PD‑1 blockade.
external_validation_needed: Trial data from the zenitatamab + tislelizumab triplet arm

crispro_opportunity.description: Leverage CrisPRO’s structural modeling to design biparatopic HER2 antibodies with optimized trans‑binding geometry, potentially surpassing zenitatamab’s CDC activity.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "different formats may mediate different potencies, but not necessarily is the case where you see particular formats with very undesirable properties"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can predict optimal epitope pairings and linker lengths to maximize clustering without inducing agonism.
external_validation_needed: Structural data of HER2 ectodomain complexes
Comparative CDC assays of engineered biparatopics

cited_competitors.name: Trastuzumab + Pertuzumab
cited_competitors.context: Standard of care combination used as benchmark for efficacy and safety

cited_competitors.name: Tislelizumab
cited_competitors.context: Checkpoint inhibitor combined with zenitatamab in a triplet regimen

unresolved_questions: What is the mechanistic basis for the loss of growth inhibition and agonism observed with the dual scFv format?
Can the CDC effect of zenitatamab be replicated with alternative biparatopic designs that avoid potential agonism?

nct_numbers_to_check: NCT04512345  // hypothetical zenitatamab phase III biliary tract trial
NCT04678901  // hypothetical zenitatamab + tislelizumab triplet trial

talk_metadata.talk_title: Two‑plus‑one (2+1) bispecific T‑cell engagers and CD28 co‑stimulatory strategies
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the two plus one format provides avidity‑driven selectivity"
Speaker said: "we observed a ~70% CRS rate during priming, but low grade‑3 and no grade‑4 events at target dose"
Speaker said: "the strongest progressors had absolutely no EMPP3 expression"
Speaker said: "we design FC‑silenced versions to avoid non‑selective T‑cell activation"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Efficacy observed in patients lacking EMPP3 expression suggests off‑target or bystander effects not fully understood
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: Speaker said: "the two strongest progressors in this study actually had absolutely no EMPP3 expression"
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Speaker said: "around a 70% CRS rate during priming"

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Avidity‑driven CD3 engagement of EMPP3‑high tumors
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: No pre‑screening for EMPP3 expression despite known heterogeneity; inclusion of EMPP3‑negative patients dilutes signal
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: EMPP3 membrane positivity as a mandatory enrollment criterion
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Small cohort (N=20) with mixed biomarker status limits statistical power to detect true efficacy

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker acknowledged that avidity‑driven selectivity is a concept previously published (e.g., Christian Kline 2014), reducing novelty
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Heavy reliance on step‑up dosing to mitigate CRS may limit dosing flexibility and patient compliance

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker noted variability in CD28 expression (e.g., down‑regulation in elderly) which could blunt efficacy
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Potential for peripheral T‑cell activation if target dependency is not strictly enforced

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker demonstrated robust tumor regression in EMPP3‑high RCC patients (observation) but also highlighted that the two best responders lacked EMPP3 expression (contradiction), suggesting an unclear mechanism of action

key_data_gaps: Correlation of EMPP3 expression with response

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop a robust EMPP3 IHC/flow cytometry assay to enrich for responders and reduce exposure of EMPP3‑negative patients.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "the two strongest progressors in this study actually had absolutely no EMPP3 expression"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model expression thresholds needed for avidity‑driven killing and define a quantitative biomarker cutoff.
external_validation_needed: ClinicalTrials.gov: retrieve patient‑level EMPP3 expression data
Pathology databases for EMPP3 IHC scoring standards

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine the EMPP3 2+1 engager with a CD28 co‑stimulatory bispecific to overcome T‑cell exhaustion observed after repeated dosing.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "co‑dosing with CD28 bispecific rescued anti‑tumor activity after chronic restimulation"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate sequential dosing schedules and predict synergistic cytokine profiles.
external_validation_needed: Preclinical data on CD28 bispecific + EMPP3 engager combos
Safety data on combined CRS risk

crispro_opportunity.description: Leverage CrisPRO to design 2+1 formats with optimized CD3 affinity tiers (medium vs high) to balance potency and CRS risk.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we have settled on the medium affinity for CD3 as the sweet spot"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can predict kinetic avidity effects of varying CD3 affinities within the 2+1 scaffold.
external_validation_needed: Biophysical binding data for CD3 variants
In‑vivo CRS incidence correlated with affinity

cited_competitors.name: Amgen AMG 509 (2+1 FGFR2 T‑cell engager)
cited_competitors.context: Cited as a successful example of the 2+1 format achieving high PSA response rates

cited_competitors.name: Zencor EMPP3 2+1 program
cited_competitors.context: Own program discussed throughout the talk

unresolved_questions: What is the mechanistic basis for tumor regressions in EMPP3‑negative patients?
Can alternative CD28 co‑stimulatory targets (e.g., CD2) improve efficacy in elderly populations with reduced CD28 expression?

nct_numbers_to_check: NCT05812345  // hypothetical Zencor EMPP3 2+1 Phase 1 trial
